metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_4027
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Figure 1C and D: DCV fusion events quantified via NPY-pHluorin assay displayed a dramatic reduction in Rab3 QKO neurons as compared to WT.
Figure 1 F-H: Re-expression of RAB3A significantly increased DCV fusion, compared to RAB3 QKO neurons denoting its role in regulating dense-core vesicle exocytosis.
Figure S1 A, B, C and D: The loss of DCV fusion in RAB3 QKO neurons was confirmed using BDNF as an independent reporter for neuromodulator release.

The paper further accounts for potential confounding factors that could, in principle, contribute to the dramatic reduction in DCV fusion.
Figure S1 F: the number of NPY-pHluorin-labeled DCVs per neuron was similar between genotypes which signify that Rab3 QKO particularly affects the DCV fusion.
Figure S1 G and H: The temporal distribution of fusion before and during stimulation was not significantly different in Rab3 QKO and WT.
S1 I: There wasn't any significant difference in the calcium dynamics between the Rab3 QKO and WT.
S1 K and S2 A-C: The location of fusion events, synaptic or extra-synaptic, did not differ between wild-type and RAB3 QKO neurons.
Figure S2 D-G: The number of synapses and the total dendritic length of wild-type, and RAB3 QKO neurons were similar.
Figure S2 H: The fluorescence intensity of VGLUT1 at synapses was not altered in RAB3 QKO neurons.
Figure S2 I-L: The number and distribution of puncta labelled with the endogenous DCV marker chromogranin B (CHGB) were similar between genotypes.
Figure S2 N: The number of CHGB puncta and total CHGB protein levels was similar between RAB3 QKO and control neurons.
Figure S2 M and S1 J: The intensity of CHGB puncta and intensity of individual DCV fusion events was significantly reduced in Rab3 QKO indicating a probable role in DCV loading.

Together, all the aforementioned data strengthens the conclusion that Rab3a is an important regulator of DCV fusion.. Experimental description: Rab3ABCD null was derived from Schlüter et al., 2004
